name: autonomous-engineering
description: Autonomous software delivery for Codex — the auto-spec → auto-ship lifecycle plus the converge-loop / adversarial-verify / checkpoint-resume / fan-out-work / budget-guard primitives, delivered as Codex-native skill adapters.

interface.shortDescription: Bounded, fail-closed autonomous delivery skills for Codex.
interface.longDescription: Turns the software delivery lifecycle into autonomous, loop- and workflow-driven phases (auto-spec → auto-plan → auto-build → auto-simplify → auto-test → auto-review → auto-performance → auto-ship) on top of durable loop, adversarial-verify, checkpoint-resume, fan-out, and budget-guard primitives. Every loop is bounded by an explicit termination set and every gate fails closed — a check that did not run is never reported clean.
defaultPrompt: Use Autonomous Engineering to take this spec through a bounded, fail-closed build-to-review pipeline.
Use Autonomous Engineering to run a converge-loop on the open review findings until the termination set fires.
